_note: Regression baseline for broadcast/iid/density shared paths. Captured at HEAD (post-PR-#44) before multi-axis surgery. ANY movement on an existing expect entry after an engine change is a regression — stop and investigate. New models are ADDED; existing entries never move. Oracle notes per entry.

density.name: prior@pt
density.oracle: Distributions.jl validated: logpdf(Normal(0,5),0) + log(2)+logpdf(Cauchy(0,5),1) [half-Cauchy] + sum_j logpdf(Normal(0,1), j) = -113.980126042153 (Δ 1.4e-14)

density.name: likelihood@pt
density.oracle: Distributions.jl validated: sum logpdf(Normal(theta_i, se_i), y_i) = -30.1834262038121 (Δ0). unnorm posterior = prior@pt + this.

density.name: Binomial-grid@p0.7
density.oracle: Distributions.jl validated: sum(logpdf(Binomial(n_data[g][k],0.7),r_data[g][k]) for g in 1:2, k in 1:16) = -85.69372710973784 (Δ 2e-14 float)

models.oracle: scipy/pyROOT analytic, T_2, verified engine==ROOT to 2e-9, 2026-06-25: pure 1+T_2 model, RooChebychev t-rescaling on [0,10]; t=(2x-10)/10; w(x)=1+T_2(t)=2t^2; Z=20/3; pdf(1)=0.192, pdf(3)=0.048, pdf(7)=0.048; analytic logL=log(0.192)+log(0.048)+log(0.048)=-7.7233684431028475; engine midpoint-quadrature error vs analytic ~4.5e-8

density.name: L@alpha=0.5
density.oracle_note: T_2 non-degenerate: analytic oracle (scipy/pyROOT) = -7.7233684431028475; engine value pinned below differs by ~4.5e-8 (midpoint-quadrature error on T_2 curvature); alpha=0.5 is a free param but cb does not depend on alpha
